reasoning: The market price as of mid-October 2025 places the probability of the <1.10ºC bracket at 1%. Global temperatures in recent years (especially 2023 and 2024) have consistently exceeded the 1.10ºC anomaly mark using the 1951-1980 GISTEMP baseline, with October 2023 reaching around +1.34ºC. Although a weak La Niña is expected by late 2025, it is highly unlikely to cool global temperatures enough to drop the anomaly below 1.10ºC. Additionally, it is highly unlikely that data publication will be delayed up to January 1, 2026, which would trigger a default resolution to this lowest bracket. Given the minimum probability constraints (0.05), I assign my forecast at 5%.
